mechanism_narrative: Persistent increases in real retail sales (RSAFS) reflect strong consumer spending, which drives corporate revenues and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of RSAFS momentum on 20-day forward returns is less than 0.03 over a 3-year window, or the predictive signal reverses sign in more than 30% of rolling sub-periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above a critical threshold of 5%, it signals labor market weakness and rising recession risk, causing investors to reduce risk exposure and driving S&P 500 lower over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where UNRATE exceeds 5%, the subsequent 20-day SPX return is positive in more than 55% of cases, or the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of high federal funds rate (tight monetary policy) and declining industrial production (contraction in output) signals a stagflation-like environment that depresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and lower earnings expectations, producing negative forward returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% and INDPRO is in a downward trend (3-month negative), the 60-day forward SPX return is positive in more than 50% of cases, or the interaction term in a regression has p-value > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: During a late-cycle expansion (unemployment rate below 4%), further declines in the unemployment rate signal an overheating economy that will prompt central bank tightening and eventual slowdown, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In instances where UNRATE is below 4%, a decrease in UNRATE is associated with positive median 20-day returns, or the correlation is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) erodes real purchasing power and increases uncertainty about future monetary policy tightening, which dampens equity valuations and leads to negative short-term S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return has a positive correlation with CPI momentum in more than 60% of rolling windows, or the information coefficient (IC) is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1400 thousand units, it signals a weakening housing market that has negative spillovers to consumer wealth and construction employment, leading to lower S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where HOUST is below 1400, the 60-day forward SPX return is positive in more than half of cases, or the signal's Sharpe ratio is less than 0.1 after considering transaction costs.

mechanism_narrative: When both nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) are rising and retail sales (RSAFS) are rising, the joint signal indicates synchronized economic strength that boosts corporate earnings and investor confidence, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-trading-day window.
failure_condition: When both PAYEMS and RSAFS are above their respective trailing 3-month averages, the subsequent 20-day return is negative more often than positive, or the interaction coefficient in a linear model is not statistically significant at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: In the early stages of a recovery from a recession, rapid increases in industrial production (INDPRO) and real GDP (GDPC1) reflect strong demand rebound and pent-up activity, which sharply boosts earnings and stock prices over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: During periods where INDPRO and GDPC1 both accelerate (>2% quarterly growth), the 60-day SPX return is negative in more than 40% of cases, or the average excess return over risk-free is less than 1%.
